summary.intent: Simplify the Dockerfile by replacing the explicit feature list with default features, avoiding maintenance drift.
summary.outcome: Dockerfile updated to use default features (dropping `--no-default-features` with explicit list), with `local-llm-metal` confirmed safe on Linux. PR #576 updated with the cleaner approach.

repo: Linux CI already builds with --all-features including local-llm-metal (line 29 of CI config), meaning Metal feature compiles fine on Linux — it just doesn't activate.
The explicit feature list in Dockerfile existed only to exclude local-llm-metal and add wasm; neither exclusion was actually necessary.

code.finding: Switched from --no-default-features with explicit list to default features; wasm inclusion and metal exclusion handled implicitly.

workflow: Before removing a feature flag, check CI config to verify the feature already compiles in the target environment.
Explicit feature lists in Dockerfiles silently drift as new default features are added upstream.

friction: Required multiple grep passes to trace local-llm-metal dependencies (llama.cpp, metal flags) before confirming Linux safety.
open_items: Verify wasm feature is still included via defaults or re-add explicitly if it turns out not to be a default feature.
